Package microui.constants

Enum Class AutoResizeMode

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Enum<AutoResizeMode>
microui.constants.AutoResizeMode
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Comparable<AutoResizeMode>, Constable
public enum AutoResizeMode extends Enum<AutoResizeMode>
Enumeration defining the scaling factors for automatic text resizing in TextView. The AutoResizeMode determines how aggressively text is scaled to fit within its bounds, with lower values resulting in larger text and higher values resulting in smaller text.

Available modes range from FULL (largest text) to TINY (smallest text).

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class java.lang.Enum

    Enum.EnumDesc<E extends Enum<E>>

  • Enum Constant Summary

    Enum Constants
    Enum Constant
    Description
    BIG
    Large text size - text occupies about half the available space.
    FULL
    Largest text size - uses the full available space.
    MIDDLE
    Medium text size - balanced size for general use.
    SMALL
    Small text size - compact text for limited space.
    TINY
    Smallest text size - minimal text for maximum content.

  • Enum Constant Details

    • FULL

      public static final AutoResizeMode FULL
      Largest text size - uses the full available space. Scaling factor: 1 (text size = min(width, height) / 1)

    • BIG

      public static final AutoResizeMode BIG
      Large text size - text occupies about half the available space. Scaling factor: 2 (text size = min(width, height) / 2)

    • MIDDLE

      public static final AutoResizeMode MIDDLE
      Medium text size - balanced size for general use. Scaling factor: 3 (text size = min(width, height) / 3)

    • SMALL

      public static final AutoResizeMode SMALL
      Small text size - compact text for limited space. Scaling factor: 4 (text size = min(width, height) / 4)

    • TINY

      public static final AutoResizeMode TINY
      Smallest text size - minimal text for maximum content. Scaling factor: 5 (text size = min(width, height) / 5)

    • getValue

      public final int getValue()
      Gets the scaling factor divisor for this resize mode. The divisor is used to calculate automatic text size:
